foundation.title: POINTER
foundation.subtitle: Memory Addresses
foundation.content: A C pointer stores the literal memory address (like 0x7ffd) of a variable. It's the key to high-level control in games and OS kernels.
foundation.scenario: A Physical Address on an Envelope. The letter (the data) is inside. The address on top (the pointer) tells you exactly which house to go to.

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Asking for 4 bytes of RAM using the '*' symbol.

examples.title: Extraction
examples.explanation: Retrieving the literal memory address using the '&' (Address of) symbol.

examples.title: The Null
examples.explanation: Assigning 'NULL' to a pointer to show it's currently pointing at nothing.

foundation.keyPoint: * = Value at. & = Address of. NULL = Empty.

foundation.title: MALLOC
foundation.subtitle: Heap Control
foundation.content: In C, you can ask for RAM yourself from the 'Heap' using malloc(). This memory stays yours until you give it back manually.
foundation.scenario: A Hotel Room. You call (malloc) and ask for a room. You can keep the room as long as you want until you Check Out (free).

examples.title: The Request
examples.explanation: Asking or raw memory by its total byte count directly from the OS.

examples.title: Check In
examples.explanation: Ensuring the system actually HAD memory for you to take or it crashes null.

examples.title: The Result
examples.explanation: Accessing the new memory address as if it were a normal local variable.

foundation.keyPoint: malloc(bytes) returns address of newly allocated RAM.

foundation.title: FREE_MEM
foundation.subtitle: Safety Returns
foundation.content: C has no Garbage Collector. If you don't 'free()' the memory you took, your computer slowly runs out of RAM (Leak).
foundation.scenario: A Library Book. If you take out 1,000 books (malloc) and never return them (free), the library will run dry and close (Crash).

examples.title: The Return
examples.explanation: Releasing the address back to the pool so other apps can use it.

examples.title: Double Free
examples.explanation: Crashing the computer by trying to free() the same address two times.

examples.title: Clean Up
examples.explanation: Setting a pointer to NULL after freeing it for ultimate safety.

foundation.keyPoint: Every malloc() must have exactly ONE free().

gameData.hint: Opens the door of the address.
